concepts.name: Linked List
concepts.short_definition: A linear data structure where elements are not stored at contiguous memory locations.

concepts.name: Node
concepts.short_definition: The basic building block of a linked list, containing data and a pointer to the next node.

concepts.name: Head
concepts.short_definition: A pointer to the first node in a linked list.

concepts.name: Tail
concepts.short_definition: A pointer to the last node in a linked list.

concepts.name: Next Pointer
concepts.short_definition: A reference within a node that points to the subsequent node in the list.

concepts.name: Traversal
concepts.short_definition: The process of visiting each node in a linked list.

concepts.name: Iteration
concepts.short_definition: A method of traversal using loops.

concepts.name: Recursion
concepts.short_definition: A method of traversal where a function calls itself.

concepts.name: Reversal
concepts.short_definition: The operation of changing the direction of pointers in a linked list.

concepts.name: Iterative Reversal
concepts.short_definition: Reversing a linked list using a loop.

concepts.name: Previous Pointer
concepts.short_definition: A pointer in a node that points to the preceding node (used in doubly linked lists).

concepts.name: Doubly Linked List
concepts.short_definition: A linked list where each node has pointers to both the next and previous nodes.

concepts.name: Recursive Reversal
concepts.short_definition: Reversing a linked list using recursion.

concepts.name: In-place Reversal
concepts.short_definition: Reversing a linked list without using extra space proportional to the list size.

concepts.name: Temporary Variable
concepts.short_definition: A variable used to store a value temporarily during an operation.

concepts.name: Current Node
concepts.short_definition: The node currently being processed during traversal or reversal.

concepts.name: Previous Node
concepts.short_definition: The node that was processed before the current node.

concepts.name: Next Node
concepts.short_definition: The node that follows the current node.

concepts.name: Pointer Manipulation
concepts.short_definition: The act of changing the values of pointers to alter the structure of a linked list.

concepts.name: Base Case (Recursion)
concepts.short_definition: The condition that stops the recursive calls.

concepts.name: Recursive Step
concepts.short_definition: The part of a recursive function that calls itself with a modified input.

concepts.name: Null Pointer
concepts.short_definition: A pointer that does not point to any valid memory location.

concepts.name: Empty List
concepts.short_definition: A linked list with no nodes.

concepts.name: Single Node List
concepts.short_definition: A linked list containing only one node.

concepts.name: Time Complexity
concepts.short_definition: A measure of the time required to execute an algorithm as a function of input size.

concepts.name: Space Complexity
concepts.short_definition: A measure of the memory required to execute an algorithm as a function of input size.

concepts.name: Algorithm
concepts.short_definition: A step-by-step procedure for solving a problem.

concepts.name: Data Structure
concepts.short_definition: A particular way of organizing and storing data in a computer.

concepts.name: Singular Linked List
concepts.short_definition: Another term for a singly linked list.

concepts.name: Reversed Head
concepts.short_definition: The new head of the linked list after reversal.

concepts.name: Reversed Tail
concepts.short_definition: The new tail of the linked list after reversal.

concepts.name: Stack (Implicit)
concepts.short_definition: The call stack used by recursive functions.

concepts.name: Three-Pointer Approach
concepts.short_definition: An iterative reversal technique using current, previous, and next pointers.

concepts.name: Two-Pointer Approach
concepts.short_definition: A reversal technique that might involve fewer explicit pointer variables.

concepts.name: Edge Cases
concepts.short_definition: Unusual or extreme conditions that an algorithm must handle.

concepts.name: Empty List Reversal
concepts.short_definition: The result of reversing an empty linked list.

concepts.name: Single Node Reversal
concepts.short_definition: The result of reversing a linked list with one node.

concepts.name: Memory Allocation
concepts.short_definition: The process of reserving memory for data.

concepts.name: Memory Deallocation
concepts.short_definition: The process of releasing reserved memory.

concepts.name: Pointer Swapping
concepts.short_definition: Exchanging the values of two pointers.
